overview: Izzet Splinter Twin is a Modern combo deck that leverages Splinter Twin to copy creatures and generate infinite value or massive damage. The provided signature cards accelerate this game plan by tutoring for combo pieces, pumping attackers, and drawing cards to stabilize against interaction. Success hinges on protecting the combo sequence until it resolves.
win_conditions: Copying a creature with Splinter Twin to create an infinite damage or card draw loop
Using Flow State and Fear of Missing Out to push lethal combat damage while drawing cards to outlast opponents
Tutoring for key combo pieces or lands with Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to close games faster

keep_criteria: Hands with Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student or Splinter Twin
Low mana value pump spells like Flow State or Fear of Missing Out
Hands containing early interaction or lands to stabilize
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably fixing), at least one combo piece (Splinter Twin or protection creature), and disruption or a Tamiyo/Flow State enabler.
mulligan_criteria: High mana curve hands with no combo pieces
Hands lacking lands or card draw
Hands with only pump spells but no creatures to attach them to
Mulligan to single-color fixing if it delays turn-2 plays, or hands with only threats and no protection/disruption.

early_game: Play Tamiyo to find Splinter Twin or a key creature
Cast pump spells on opponent's turn to protect them
Establish board presence and draw cards to find combo pieces
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Spell Pierce/Thoughtseize if possible, or set up with Tamiyo if mana allows.
Turn 2: Cast Goblin Electromancer/Ironshell Crab or Splinter Twin, using Flow State or Fear of Missing Out to dig for protection or combo pieces.
Prioritize establishing the combo threat while holding up counterspells/removal for turn-2 interaction.
mid_game: Cast Splinter Twin to copy a creature with a relevant ability
Sequence pump spells to maximize combat damage or card draw
Begin combo loops or apply pressure to force opponent into mistakes
Preserve counterspells for critical combo pieces or opponent threats.
Use Flow State to dig for protection or combo pieces when running low.
Manage Tamiyo loyalty carefully to maintain card advantage and protection.
Avoid overcommitting mana on non-essential spells.
Commit threats only when you have protection or can trade favorably.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers or hard removal.
late_game: Stabilize with card advantage from pump spells
Execute infinite damage or draw loops to close the game
Use remaining resources to protect the combo from late-game interaction
Splinter Twin paired with a protection creature (Ironshell Crab/Goblin Electromancer) or a direct damage spell.
Use Flow State to dig for the final piece or Fear of Missing Out to set up a lethal turn.
Go all-in when you have protection, enough mana for a lethal blow, and the opponent has no sweepers or hard removal in hand.
Also commit when you can out-value them with Tamiyo's ability or Flow State's library advantage.

key_cards.name: Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student
key_cards.notes: Use to find Splinter Twin, key creatures, or lands. Exile her to avoid removal and keep the combo on track.

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.notes: Cast on opponent's turn to protect it. Attach to Twin copies or attackers to push damage and draw cards.

key_cards.name: Fear of Missing Out
key_cards.notes: Synergizes with Flow State for massive damage spikes. Use to race aggro or draw into answers against control.

key_cards.name: Splinter Twin
key_cards.notes: Copy a creature to activate infinite loops or massive board swings. Sequence after pump spells to maximize impact.

sequencing_tips: Hold pump spells until the opponent's turn to avoid counterspells
Cast Tamiyo before Twin to ensure you have the copy target or necessary lands
Sequence Twin after pump spells are on the stack or resolved to maximize combat damage
Prioritize drawing cards with pump spells to find answers to interaction
Common mistake: Overcommitting to the combo without protection.
Always hold up interaction for turn-2 threats.
Flow State requires careful sequencing—don't cast it without a plan to dig for protection or combo pieces.
Tamiyo's loyalty abilities are crucial for card advantage and protection, so don't exhaust them prematurely.
Fear of Missing Out is best used to set up your next turn or dig for a specific piece.
Watch for opponent's sideboard cards, especially artifact hate against Affinity and sweepers against control.

matchups.advice: Race with pump spells and early Twin copies. Use Tamiyo to find lands or early interaction.
watch_for: Hate cards that tax spells or creatures
Fast damage that outpaces your combo setup

matchups.advice: Protect your combo pieces with pump spells and card draw. Sequence Twin carefully to avoid counters.
Apply steady pressure with pump spells and Twin copies. Outvalue them with card draw.
Use Fear of Missing Out to dig around counters, cast threats at end step, or use Tamiyo to protect key pieces. Employ cheap, uncounterable threats or split your combo across multiple turns.
Apply early pressure to race their big spells. Use disruption to steal their mana rocks or counterspell their ramp spells. Prioritize removing their key ramp pieces before they can stabilize.

Win the resource war by out-drawing with Tamiyo and Flow State. Prioritize disruption to steal their combo pieces or counter their key spells. Control the board with efficient removal and counterspells.

common_threats: Counterspells that disrupt the combo sequence
Board wipes that clear Twin and pump spell targets
Hate cards that tax spell casting or creature abilities
Fast aggro decks that race the combo
Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, Boros Charm), hard removal (Path to Exile, Swords to Plowshares), and combo finishers.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells or hold up interaction.
Track graveyard contents for reanimation or storm threats.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Archetype name and signature cards indicate a copy-based combo strategy accelerated by pump spells and tutoring.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards provide tutoring, combat pump, and card draw, which synergize with Twin's copy mechanic to form a win condition.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Standard combo piloting principles applied to the provided pump spells and tutor.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: General Modern format threats that interact with combo/tempo strategies.
